math_lines: Stage 1 (2 NaOH + CO2 -> Na2CO3): 8.7757 mol Na2CO3 generated
Residuals -> NaOH 0.0000e+00 mol, Na2CO3 0.0000 mol, NaHCO3 17.5513 mol
Henderson-Hasselbalch with pKa2 = 10.329 informs CO3/HCO3 ratios.
CO2 for ratio shift = 5.4089 mol; for NaOH neutralization = 0.0000 mol
notes: Carbon ledger includes dissolved CO2 beyond solid conversions.
Final product pH implies [CO3]/[HCO3] ≈ 0.458 (>1 carbonate-rich buffer, <1 bicarbonate-rich).
reaction_guidance.summary:
Initial NaOH charge: 702.0 g (17.5513 mol)
CO2 absorbed to date: 817.2 g (18.5689 mol)
Ledger -> Na2CO3 0.0000 mol, NaHCO3 17.5513 mol, dissolved CO2 1.0176 mol
Add 238.04 g CO2 (5.4089 mol) to approach pH 8.00 (predicted ~= 8.13).
